text: People reading this should be aware that the data reflects only SPOT PRICING, which SemiAnalysis clarified in the 2nd part of this multi-thread post.  To understand what's spot pricing, just read Amazon's AWS page  https:// aws.amazon.com/compare/the-di fference-between-on-demand-instances-and-reserved-instances/ …

I respected SemiAnalysis tremendously until about a year ago. Now your analysis is biased, e.g., you use rising spot prices as evidence of tight supply, but dismiss the same signal when it points downwards. It makes me believe you're no longer a serious publication.
Did you even read the thread? Semianalysis clearly states that long term pricing is going up.

SemiAnalysis calls the 7th-gen TPU “arguably on par with Nvidia Blackwell” , and argues TPUs reach higher realized model-FLOPs-utilization than Blackwell because Nvidia’s marketed peak FLOPs are inflated by clock speeds the chips can’t sustain (Hopper 80% of peak, Blackwell 70%)
